'''Greg Eden''' (born 14 November 1990) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er or {{rlp|fb}} for the [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

Eden has represented the [[England Knights]] at international level. He has previously played for the [[Castleford Tigers]], the [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]], the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[NRL]], and the [[Halifax Panthers]], [[Featherstone Rovers]], [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]] and [[North Wales Crusaders]] in the [[RFL Championship]]. He has spent time on loan and dual registration at the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the Super League and at the [[Newcastle Thunder|Gateshead Thunder]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]].

Eden is renowned for his lightning pace and his prolific finishing ability.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=2022-04-03 |title=Castleford Tigers' Greg Eden – the most underrated winger in Super League? |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/castleford-tigers-greg-eden-the-most-underrated-winger-in-super-league-51016/ |access-date=2022-04-14 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=2017-03-17 |title=Wayne Bennett keeping tabs on Greg Eden ahead of World Cup |url=https://www.totalrl.com/wayne-bennett-keeping-tabs-greg-eden-ahead-world-cup/ |access-date=2022-04-14 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> He ranks as Castleford's second-highest top tryscorer in the Super League era, and eighth-highest of all time.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Castleford Tigers Team Directory |url=https://stats.rugbyleaguerecords.com/teamdirectory.php?tselect=58 |access-date=20 August 2023 |website=RL Record Keepers' Club}}</ref>

==Background==
Eden was born in [[Castleford]], West Yorkshire, England.

== Club career ==
=== Castleford Tigers ===
Eden made his senior debut for Castleford against the [[Warrington Wolves]] on 1 July 2011, scoring and assisting a try.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/13995/7014033/king-treble-tames-tigers|title=King treble tames Tigers|website=Sky Sports|language=en|access-date=2020-03-31}}</ref>

=== Huddersfield Giants ===
Eden signed for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] from the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the winter of 2011 and made an immediate impact in the [[Super League]], establishing himself as first choice {{rlp|fb}} at the Huddersfield Giants within weeks of the season beginning. He was rewarded with a long-term deal until the end of the 2015 season.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/18248397|title=Huddersfield Giants full-back Greg Eden pens new deal|date=2012-05-29|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-03-30|language=en-gb}}</ref>

=== Hull Kingston Rovers ===
Eden moved to [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the winter of 2012, signing a three-year deal.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/20319273|title=Hull KR sign Greg Eden from Huddersfield Giants|date=2012-11-14|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-03-30|language=en-gb}}</ref> He made 39 appearances for the club across 2 seasons, scoring 24 tries.

====Salford Red Devils (loan)====
[[File:Greg Eden.jpg|thumb|right|Eden playing for the Salford Red Devils in 2014]]
On 25 April 2014, Greg Eden joined the [[Salford Red Devils]] on a 1-month loan deal.<ref name="superleague">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/30067/eden-joins-salford-ranks|title=Eden joins Salford ranks|publisher=superleague.co.uk|access-date=28 May 2014}}</ref><ref name="bbc">{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/27162270|title=Salford Red Devils: Greg Eden joins on loan from Hull KR|work=BBC Sport|access-date=28 May 2014}}</ref> In May, this was subsequently extended on a week-by-week basis, with Hull KR able to recall him at any chosen period.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/greg-eden-extends-loan-deal-salford-red-devils/|title=Greg Eden extends loan deal at Salford Red Devils|last=Bower|first=Aaron|date=2014-05-22|website=Total Rugby League - TotalRL.com {{!}} League Express {{!}} Rugby League World|language=en-US|access-date=2020-03-30}}</ref> The Robins exercised this right in June following a serious injury to fullback [[Ben Cockayne]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12217/9340081/super-league-hull-kr-recall-greg-eden-from-his-loan-with-salford-city-red-devils|title=Super League: Hull KR recall Greg Eden from his loan with Salford City Red Devils|website=Sky Sports|language=en|access-date=2020-03-30}}</ref>

==== Gateshead Thunder (dual registration) ====
Eden made 2 appearances for [[Newcastle Thunder|Gateshead]] in 2014, through their dual-registration arrangement with Hull KR.{{cn|date=March 2023}}

===Brisbane Broncos===
On 1 September 2014, Eden signed with [[Brisbane Broncos]], boosting their {{rlp|FB}} ranks after the Broncos showed great interest in him.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/fullback-options-rise-for-broncos-following-signing-of-english-ace-greg-eden/story-fniabm4i-1227044257677|title=Fullback options rise for Broncos|newspaper=Courier Mail|access-date=18 February 2017}}</ref> Eden spent the 2015 season with the Broncos affiliate club [[Wynnum Manly Seagulls]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Willacy |first1=Gavin |title=Greg Eden interview: 'My target this year is to crack the NRL with the Broncos' |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/no-helmets-required/2016/feb/18/greg-eden-brisbane-broncos-wigan-world-club-series-england |website=The Guardian |access-date=25 January 2022 |date=18 February 2016}}</ref> In February 2016, Eden made his Broncos debut in the [[2016 World Club Series|World Club Series]] against the [[Wigan Warriors]], scoring a try in a 42–12 win.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Newsum |first1=Matt |title=World Club Series: Wigan Warriors 12-42 Brisbane Broncos |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/35588934 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=25 January 2022 |date=20 February 2016}}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers===
In June 2016 Eden agreed upon a two-year contract with hometown [[Super League]] side [[Castleford Tigers]] for the upcoming season starting in 2017.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/36518419|title=Greg Eden: Castleford Tigers re-sign Brisbane Broncos full-back|date=2016-06-13|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-03-30|language=en-gb}}</ref>

Eden was given the squad number 5 prior to the start of the season, and played the majority of matches on Castleford's left wing. He scored 38 Super League tries in his first season back at Castleford, including 5 hat-tricks against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] twice, [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]], [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] and [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]. Eden topped the try scoring charts and was named in the [[Super League Dream Team]] for this season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/51080/eight-debutants-in--betfred-super-league-dream-team|title=Eight debutants in 2017 Betfred Super League Dream Team|website=www.rugby-league.com|access-date=2020-03-30}}</ref> He played at fullback in the [[2017 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]], after [[Zak Hardaker]] was suspended two days before the game following a failed drugs test.<ref name="Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2017/oct/07/castleford-v-leeds-grand-final-2017-live-super-league-rugby?page=with:block-59d9128de4b00593b4d67a05#liveblog-navigation|title=Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=7 October 2017|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/41536394|title=Grand Final 2017: Castleford 6-24 Leeds Rhinos|date=7 October 2017|work=BBC Sport|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/oct/07/castleford-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Danny McGuire guides Leeds to Grand Final success over Castleford|date=7 October 2017|work=Guardian|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41542423|title=Zak Hardaker: Castleford Tigers full-back banned after positive drugs test|date=2017-10-09|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-03-30|language=en-gb}}</ref>

Eden's excellent strike rate for the Tigers was rewarded with a three-year contract extension in 2018.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=5873|title=Three more years For Eden!|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2020-03-30}}</ref> However, his season was limited to just 15 appearances following a hamstring injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=5915|title=Injury update|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2020-03-30}}</ref> Despite this, he still scored 18 tries, including 3 hat-tricks against [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]], [[Catalans Dragons|Catalans]] and [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]].

In the first match of 2020, Eden scored a trademark length-of-the-field try against [[Toronto Wolfpack]], before hyper extending his knee in the second half.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=6787|title=Tigers issue an Injury Update|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2020-03-30}}</ref> He scored a hat-trick against [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]] and finished the season with 8 tries in 8 appearances.

Eden's impressive performances following his return to the team towards the end of an injury-hit [[Super League XXVI|2021 season]] contributed towards Castleford's improvement in form.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Craven |first=Dave |date=2021-08-24 |title=Castleford Tigers winger Greg Eden working to improve the subtle areas of his game |work=The Yorkshire Post |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-winger-greg-eden-working-to-improve-the-subtle-areas-of-his-game-3357132 |access-date=2022-03-17}}</ref> He scored a late length-of-the-field interception try on 12 August 2021 to seal Castleford's first league win away to [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in 31 years.<ref>{{cite web |date=2021-08-12 |title=Super League: St Helens 10-20 Castleford Tigers - Cas win to end 31-year wait |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58193553 |access-date=2022-03-17 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

Eden scored a hat-trick against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]] in his first appearance of the 2022 season.<ref>{{cite web |date=2022-03-06 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 33-26 Hull FC - Cas edge out Black and Whites to claim first win of the season |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/60555428 |access-date=2022-03-17 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He was also voted the Fans' Man of the Match for his first 2 games of the year.<ref>{{cite tweet|author=Castleford Tigers|author-link=Castleford Tigers|user=CTRLFC|number=1501543575170990081|title=After counting up your votes, the @HuntersHomes Fans Man of the Match from our victory over Hull FC is... @GregEden1}}</ref><ref>{{cite tweet|author=Castleford Tigers|author-link=Castleford Tigers|user=CTRLFC|number=1503329072767717380|title=We counted up your comments and the @HuntersHomes Fans Man of the Match from our away match at Huddersfield Giants is @GregEden1. Back-to-back weeks for the winger}}</ref> He scored his 100th Castleford try on 14 April against [[Wakefield Trinity]] in his 94th game for the club.<ref name="Try 100">{{Cite web |last=Newsum |first=Matt |date=2022-04-14 |title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity 4-34 Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/61113166 |access-date=2022-04-14 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Ibbetson |first=Stephen |date=17 April 2022 |title=Greg Eden battles adversity to reach Castleford Tigers try landmark |url=https://www.totalrl.com/eden-battles-adversity-to-reach-tigers-landmark/ |access-date=20 August 2023 |website=Total RL}}</ref> In the second half of the season, Eden was primarily utilised as a {{Rlp|so}} or {{Rlp|fb}} due to the concurrent injury absences of [[Jake Trueman]], [[Gareth O'Brien]], [[Niall Evalds]] and [[Ryan Hampshire]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Ibbetson |first=Stephen |date=17 July 2022 |title=Castleford Tigers winger Greg Eden reveals secret to keeping himself fit after career worries |url=https://www.totalrl.com/water-keeps-eden-project-going/ |access-date=20 August 2023 |website=Total RL}}</ref> In July, he signed a new one-year deal with Cas, with the option of a further year.<ref>{{Cite web |date=14 July 2022 |title=Greg Eden: Castleford Tigers versatile back signs new one-year deal |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62157079 |access-date=20 August 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

In round 22 of the 2023 Super League season, Eden scored a hat-trick against [[Wakefield Trinity]] to reach 150 career tries. Castleford would win the match 28-12.<ref>{{Cite web |date=18 August 2023 |title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity 12-28 Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66523041 |access-date=20 August 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=18 August 2023 |title=Wakefield Trinity 12-28 Castleford Tigers: Greg Eden hat-trick gives Cas edge in Super League relegation battle |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/wakefield-vs-castleford/58383 |access-date=20 August 2023 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Craven |first=David |date=18 August 2023 |title=Greg Eden hat-trick sees classy Castleford Tigers win huge relegation showdown |url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-trinity-castleford-tigers-relegation-30738257 |access-date=20 August 2023 |website=The Mirror}}</ref>
Eden played 15 games for Castleford in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season and scored nine tries as the club finished 11th on the table narrowly avoiding relegation.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-ace-alex-mellor-delivers-brutally-honest-assessment-of-awful-season-weve-massively/|title=Castleford Tigers ace Alex Mellor delivers brutally honest assessment of 'awful' season; 'We've massively underperformed'|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=22 September 2023 }}</ref>

===Halifax Panthers===
On 7 November 2023, it was reported that he had signed for [[Halifax Panthers|Halifax]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=7 Nov 2023|title=Castleford Tigers speedster Greg Eden makes Championship move on two-year deal|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-speedster-greg-eden-makes-championship-halifax-panthers-move}}</ref> He made his debut for the club on 28 January 2024 in an [[RFL 1895 Cup|1895 Cup]] first round fixture away at [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham RLFC]], scoring 2 tries in a 24-20 defeat.<ref>{{Cite web |last=RLFC |first=Oldham |date=2024-01-28 |title=Oldham edge past cup holders Halifax in 24–20 Win |url=https://roughyeds.co.uk/2024/01/oldham-edge-past-cup-holders-halifax-in-2420-win/ |access-date=2024-04-25 |website=roughyeds.co.uk |language=en-GB}}</ref>

On 15 May 2024 it was announced that he had ended his contract with [[Halifax Panthers]] with immediate effect after expressing a desire to play closer to home.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=15 May 2024|title=Greg Eden released from Halifax Panthers immediately|url=https://www.totalrl.com/greg-eden-released-from-haliax-panthers-immediately/}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers===
On 19 May 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=19 May 2024|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Featherstone Rovers snap up ex-Castleford Tigers ace following release from fellow Championship club|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/featherstone-rovers-snap-up-ex-castleford-tigers-ace-following-release-from-fellow-championship-club}}</ref>

On 13 January 2025 Greg confirmed on Instagram that he had left [[Featherstone Rovers]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=13 Jan 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Ex-Castleford Tigers star Greg Eden leaves Featherstone Rovers|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-castleford-tigers-star-greg-eden-leaves-featherstone-rovers/}}</ref>

===Hunslet RLFC===
On 24 Jan 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Hunslet RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=24 Jan 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Greg Eden lands club for 2025 as Championship side herald ‘major coup’|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/greg-eden-lands-club-for-2025-as-championship-side-herald-major-coup}}</ref>

===North Wales Crusaders===
On 9 December 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[North Wales Crusaders]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=9 Dec 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Former Super League stalwart makes cross-Championship move to reunite with coach for 2026|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/greg-eden-joins-north-wales-crusaders-for-2026}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls (loan)===
On 23 April 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]] on loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=23 Apr 2026|author=Matthew Shaw|title=Greg Eden makes surprise Super League return with Bradford Bulls|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/greg-eden-makes-surprise-super-league-return-with-bradford-bulls}}</ref>

On 18 May 2026 it was confirmed that Eden's loan period would not be extended and he would return to [[North Wales Crusaders]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=18 May 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Bradford Bulls make decision on Greg Eden loan deal ahead of Warrington Wolves clash|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/bradford-greg-eden-decision-18-05-2026}}</ref>

===Sheffield Eagles===
On 3 June 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

== International career ==
In September 2012 Eden was selected in the [[England Knights]] squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.examiner.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/huddersfield-giants-greg-eden-receives-4942405|title=Huddersfield Giants' Greg Eden receives England Knights call up|last=YorkshireLive|date=2012-09-03|website=YorkshireLive|access-date=2020-03-30}}</ref>

Eden made his international début for England Knights on 28 October 2012 in the 62-24 [[2012 European Cup (rugby league)|Alitalia Cup]] series win over [[Scotland Rugby League|Scotland]] at the [[Meggetland Sports Complex|Meggetland Stadium]] in Edinburgh.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/england-knights-unchanged-for-scotland-clash/|title=England Knights unchanged for Scotland clash|last=Gordon|first=James|date=2012-10-26|website=Love Rugby League|language=en-GB|access-date=2020-03-30}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/20110487|title=Alitalia European Cup: Scotland 24-62 England Knights|date=2012-10-28|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-03-30|language=en-gb}}</ref>

==Records and achievements==
Eden scored his 100th try for Castleford on 14 April 2022 against [[Wakefield Trinity]], becoming the 12th player to achieve this in the club's history and just the 2nd in the Super League era.<ref name="Try 100" /> He reached this figure after just 94 appearances for the Tigers, making him the fastest player to reach a century for the club.

Eden's 100 tries for Castleford in 94 appearances places him second for the fastest century at a single club in [[Super League]], behind [[Josh Charnley]]'s 100 in 91 games for the [[Wigan Warriors]].

Castleford's most [[Try (rugby)|tries]] scored in a match record is 5-tries, and is jointly held by; [[Derek Foster (rugby league)|Derek Foster]] against [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]] on 10 November 1972, [[John Joyner]] against [[Millom R.L.F.C.|Millom]] on 16 September 1973, [[Steve Fenton (rugby league)|Stephen Fenton]] against [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] on 27 January 1978, [[Ian French]] against [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]] on 9 February 1986, [[St. John Ellis]] against [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] on 10 December 1989, and Greg Eden against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] on 11 June 2017.<ref name=":0">{{cite web|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/club.php?show=records|title=Castleford Tigers RLFC|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2020-03-30}}</ref> Eden became the first player to achieve this for Castleford in the Super League era.

Eden's 41 tries for Castleford in 2017 places him second for the most tries scored in a single season for Castleford, behind [[Denny Solomona|Denny Solomona's]] 42 in 2016, ahead of [[St. John Ellis|St. John Ellis']] 40 in 1993–94.<ref name=":0" />

Eden's 38 Super League tries in 2017 places him second for the most tries scored in a single Super League season, behind [[Denny Solomona|Denny Solomona's]] 40 in 2016, ahead of [[Lesley Vainikolo|Lesley Vainikolo's]] 36 in 2004.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/target-old-trafford-sees-castleford-tigers-greg-eden-put-try-record-one-side-598926|title=Target of Old Trafford sees Castleford Tigers' Greg Eden put try record to one side|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk|language=en|access-date=2020-03-30}}</ref>

Eden holds the record for fastest hat-trick in rugby league history; his treble against [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] on 29 May 2017 took just 4 minutes 59 seconds, beating [[Shaun Johnson|Shaun Johnson's]] record 6 minutes 30 seconds for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] against the [[Canberra Raiders]] in 2013.<ref name=":1">{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-hat-trick-star-greg-eden-now-eyes-denny-solomonas-try-record-1776236|title=Castleford Tigers' hat-trick star Greg Eden now eyes Denny Solomona's try record|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk|language=en|access-date=2020-03-30}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.menstuff.co.za/uncategorized/shaun-johnson-fastest-ever-hat-trick-in-rugby-league/|title=Shaun Johnson: fastest ever hat-trick in rugby league|last=Proome|first=Jeremy|date=2013-09-02|website=MenStuff|language=en-GB|access-date=2020-03-30}}</ref>

Eden is believed to hold the record for the most consecutive hat-tricks; in 2017, he scored trebles in 4 successive matches (against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] and [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]]), which is believed to be a first in the professional game.<ref name=":1" />
